description_long: This is a roughly hewn stone fragment with a prominent chevron or herringbone pattern carved into one of its broadest faces. The stone is irregularly shaped, with jagged and broken edges, suggesting it was once part of a larger architectural or decorative element. Its surface is predominantly rough, with visible striations and weathering marks, and the carved pattern is deeply incised, creating a strong visual texture. The coloration is a mix of earthy grays and browns, with some lighter and darker patches indicating mineral variation or exposure to the elements. The reverse and side faces are more natural and unworked, showing the raw, fractured stone. This object would likely be encountered as a remnant or obstacle in an urban park, historical site, or along a landscaped pathway.
description: A jagged, weathered stone fragment with a deep chevron pattern carved into one face, showing rough, natural edges and earthy coloration.
description_view_0: The front face displays a bold, deeply carved chevron pattern with pronounced ridges and grooves.
description_view_1: This angled side view highlights the stone's irregular, fractured edge and the continuation of the carved lines onto the side.
description_view_2: The rear view shows a mostly unworked, rough stone surface with natural texture and no visible carvings.
description_view_3: This side view reveals the stone's layered, striated surface and the transition from carved to natural stone.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Deeply incised chevron or herringbone pattern on one face, regular and geometric, contrasting with the natural stone texture.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible surface erosion and minor chipping, indicating prolonged exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smoother, man-made surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; could be partially surrounded by soil or low vegetation.
